meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Client Workflow for AI App Developers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn an expert client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development and build professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging gridlock, shift from reactive fixing to a structured client-side workflow. By integrating Antigravity for isolated environment simulation and Claude Code for iterative diagnostic reasoning, you transform obscure errors into logical, manageable tasks. This systematic approach ensures your code remains stable while you scale your feature set.
content.intro:
You are deep in the flow, building a feature that will define your portfolio, when the terminal turns red. The error is cryptic, the stack trace is a labyrinth, and the clock is ticking. You feel that familiar friction—the creeping doubt that maybe you aren't ready for this level of engineering.

But here is the secret: elite engineers aren't people who never encounter bugs; they are people who have a rigorous, repeatable workflow to dismantle them. Today, we are moving past trial-and-error. We are implementing a professional-grade client workflow that turns your debugger into your most powerful architectural tool.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure State
steps.description: Before fixing, define the exact state where the code breaks. We use Antigravity to create a sandboxed reproduction script that isolates the logic from the global application state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current stack trace and generate a minimal reproduction script that isolates the specific function causing the crash.

steps.title: Automated Diagnostic Mapping
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map the dependency graph of the failing component. Understanding what variables are injected versus defined locally is key to solving scope-related bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the data flow for the failing function and highlight any external dependencies or state mutations occurring before the error.

steps.title: Inject Defensive Assertions
steps.description: Implement runtime checks to validate data schemas before they reach the logic layer. This prevents silent failures and makes debugging significantly faster.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate defensive validation schemas for the function input parameters to ensure data integrity during execution.

steps.title: Iterative Logic Refinement
steps.description: With the environment stable, use Claude Code to perform a dry-run execution of the corrected logic to ensure no regression in secondary features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the proposed fix for the bug and verify it doesn't introduce circular dependencies or performance bottlenecks.

steps.title: Final Integration and Verification
steps.description: Merge the fix into the main branch, verifying that the client-side state machine remains consistent with the updated backend logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify the fix against the primary application entry point to ensure full system integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'fix: resolved critical logic bug in client workflow'

pro_tips: Always treat the stack trace as a suggestion, not a truth; verify variables manually using print-debugging at the entry point of the function.
Use Claude Code to explain the 'why' behind the error, not just the fix; this builds your mental model for future architecture.
If a bug persists for more than 30 minutes, switch to a completely different context or take a break; cognitive bias causes you to overlook the same line repeatedly.
Document your debugging process in a 'dev-log' file to create a personal knowledge base of recurring patterns in your code.
Configure Antigravity to auto-save snapshots of your environment before applying any AI-generated patches.
content.next_steps: After mastering this workflow, apply it to your existing project to refactor one legacy module, then share your results in our community forum.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to solve any bug that stands between you and your career goals. Don't let your code sit broken—apply these tactics, document your wins, and move to the next feature with absolute certainty. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
